content:
# Reservoir Engineering

**Summary**: Broad research theme addressed in the thesis collection.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2023-alzayer|Alzayer, 2023]] contributed to the theme of Reservoir Engineering

- [[2023-al-garni|Al-Garni, 2023]] investigated matrix acidizing as a method for well stimulation.

- [[2024-mohamed|Mohamed, 2024]] applied reservoir engineering principles to evaluate flowback additives for improved production in tight gas wells.

- [[2024-abdallah|Abdallah, 2024]] applied reservoir engineering principles to develop TCFs as an effective cleanup method for fractured reservoirs.

## Key Subtopics



- [[2023-al-garni|Al-Garni, 2023]] investigated matrix acidizing as a method for well stimulation.

- [[2024-mohamed|Mohamed, 2024]] applied reservoir engineering principles to evaluate flowback additives for improved production in tight gas wells.

- [[2024-abdallah|Abdallah, 2024]] applied reservoir engineering principles to develop TCFs as an effective cleanup method for fractured reservoirs.

## Methods and Approaches Used



## Key Findings Across Sources

- contributed to the theme of Reservoir Engineering ([[2023-alzayer|Alzayer, 2023]])

- Acid treatment has the greatest impact on the hardness of chalk samples. ([[2023-al-garni|Al-Garni, 2023, p. 2]])

- Ionic liquids were found to be effective in reducing IFT and improving wettability, which are key goals in reservoir engineering. ([[2024-mohamed|Mohamed, 2024]])

- Coreflood experiments were used to assess TCFs as a cleanup fluid for various fracturing systems. ([[2024-abdallah|Abdallah, 2024, p. 132]])
